supporting_evidence: The unique 'flashing' color grading by Vilmos Zsigmond, which creates a pervasive, desaturated, brownish-gold filter over the entire film, evoking an antique photograph.
The production design of the frontier town, with its unvarnished wooden buildings, muddy streets, and interiors lit with warm, dim light, all contributing to the dusty, aged aesthetic.
Iconic promotional materials and film stills consistently feature the desaturated, warm, amber-toned palette as the film's defining visual characteristic.
